title: Visual editor: "Add segment" duplicates last position and the new segment is not editable
author: fred-clement-91
body:
**Version:** v2.4.11 · HA 2026.7.1

**Bug**
In the visual editor's Segments section, "Add segment" creates a new segment with the same position value as the last one. Rows are keyed by position value, so the duplicate can't be edited (its position field won't change).

**Repro**
1. Card with pos-segments, e.g. positions 8 / 10 / 20 / 40.
2. Visual editor → Segments → "Add segment".
3. New segment is created at position 40 (same as last); its position can't be modified.

**Expected**
New segment gets a distinct default position (e.g. last + step) and is editable.

**Workaround**
Set a unique position via the YAML editor.

title: Needle slow to animate in Docker Container due to "Titles" format
author: andytf02-beep
body:
### The problem

Needles take a number of seconds to update in a Docker Container. I have a gauge and it can take 5-10 seconds for the needle to move if the title is plain text but it's fine if it's a template. I do not have the issue on HAOS.

I have provided my yaml below with my octopus account information removed. This isn't limited to the octopus integration, I can reproduce the same behaviour with entity: sensor.system_monitor_processor_temperature

If I have more than one gauge with the issue on one dashboard, all the gauges with the bug update at the same time.

### Expectation

Needle should animate almost instantly in all environments.

### YAML configuration (consider this required!)

```yaml
type: custom:gauge-card-pro
segments:
  - pos: 1200
    color: "#FF0000"
  - pos: 1000
    color: "#FFA500"
  - pos: 600
    color: rgb(255, 255, 0)
  - pos: 0
    color: var(--green-color)
needle: true
gradient: true
titles:
  primary:
    value: Home Mini
gradient_resolution: auto
max: 1500
min: 0
round: small
header: Power Use
visibility:
  - condition: numeric_state
    below: 1501
entity: sensor.octopus_energy_electricity_********_********_current_demand
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4bbf375-2240-4e35-8e07-5226a3f554ca

### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us?

```txt
No
```

### Additional information

I see that a similar issue has been raised before and closed.

Here's my situation. I have two HA instances. One is running on it's own on a Mini PC. The other is in a Docker container. I only have this issue on the Docker instance. What I think is happening is that the animation of the needle waits for the entity to update, and then the needle moves.

What I have found is that the error is caused by the way that the title is written. If is is a string, the card is slow to update, if it's a template then it's fine, see below.

This works as expected:-

titles:
  primary:
    value: "{{ \"Home Mini\" }}"

This doesn't:-

titles:
  primary:
    value: Home Mini

Looking at other similar reports, the yaml also seems to have the title as text rather than a template. Maybe the visual editor could just create the title as a template?

Hope this helps.

title: Secondary gauge - severity mode has issues with rendering
author: Jenik5
body: ### The problem Great card! In severity mode, there is an issue with the opacity setting for the secondary gauge. Setting the opacity to 0 makes only the gradient transparent, but the background remains white. This can be partially fixed using a card-mod, but the result isn't perfect. Of course, the issue becomes apparent on a dark background. ### Expectation Real transparency on the secondary gauge. ### YAML configuration (consider this required!) ```yaml type: custom:gauge-card-pro grid_options: columns: 6 rows: auto entity: sensor.battery_state_of_charge entity2: sensor.battery_power entity_id: - sensor.battery_state_of_charge - sensor.battery_power - sensor.battery_temperature - sensor.battery_state_of_health min: 0 max: 100 round: full severity_color_mode: gradient segments: - from: 0 color: "#f44336" - from: 20 color: "#ff9800" - from: 50 color: "#ffeb3b" - from: 80 color: "#4caf50" inner: min: -10 max: 10 mode: severity severity_centered: true severity_color_mode: basic gradient_background: true round: full value: "{{ ((states('sensor.battery_power') | float(0)) / 1000) | round(3) }}" segments: - from: -10 color: "#4caf50" - from: 0 color: "#9e9e9e" - from: 0.001 color: "#f44336" gradient_background_opacity: 0 gradient_resolution: auto titles: primary: value: Stav nabití secondary: value: >- {% set p = states('sensor.battery_power') | float(0) %} {{ 'Nabíjení' if p < 0 else 'Vybíjení' if p > 0 else 'Bez toku' }} color: >- {% set p = states('sensor.battery_power') | float(0) %} {{ '#4caf50' if p < 0 else '#f44336' if p > 0 else 'var(--secondary-text-color)' }} value_texts: primary: value: "{{ states('sensor.battery_state_of_charge') | float(0) | round(0) | int }}" unit_of_measurement: "%" secondary: value: >- {{ (((states('sensor.battery_power') | float(0)) / 1000) | abs) | round(2) }} unit_of_measurement: kW color: >- {% set p = states('sensor.battery_power') | float(0) %} {{ '#4caf50' if p < 0 else '#f44336' if p > 0 else 'var(--secondary-text-color)' }} icons: left: type: template value: >- {% set raw = states('sensor.battery_state_of_health') %} {% set soh = raw | float(0) %} {% set valid = raw not in ['unknown', 'unavailable', 'none'] %} {% set label = ((soh | round(0) | int | string) ~ '%') if valid else '—' %} {{ { "icon": "mdi:heart-pulse", "color": "var(--disabled-text-color)" if not valid else "#4caf50" if soh >= 80 else "#ff9800" if soh >= 60 else "#f44336", "label": label } }} tap_action: action: more-info entity: sensor.battery_state_of_health right: type: template value: |- {% set raw = states('sensor.battery_temperature') %} {% set temp = raw | float(0) %} {% set valid = raw not in ['unknown', 'unavailable', 'none'] %} {% set label = ((temp | round(1) | string) ~ ' °C') if valid else '—' %} {{ { "icon": "mdi:thermometer", "color": "var(--disabled-text-color)" if not valid else "#f44336" if temp >= 45 else "#ff9800" if temp >= 35 else "#2196f3" if temp <= 5 else "#4caf50", "label": label } }} tap_action: action: more-info entity: sensor.battery_temperature tap_action: action: more-info entity: sensor.battery_state_of_charge needle: false gradient: true gradient_resolution: auto ``` ### Screenshots ### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information _No response_

title: Add Lower buttons for non- climate functions.
author: BacchusIX
body: First off, I really like this card and will be moving my drip irrigation system to it. I always hated the look of the gauge card but found it useful for quick glancing through and checking moisture levels. So thank you for an updated look. I also really like the buttons on the bottom of the climate version and it could be awesome to do something like this for other, non climate functions ( like zone control and timer functions for my particular case). I don't know if this was a limitation of gauge card or if you were only able to program the climate version, but expanding the functionality of these buttons would make this card even more useful as a stand alone. If not, not a big deal. Again, thanks for your work on this.

title: Add an option to have the exact same gauges, but in horizontal bars.
author: cfsebastienle
body:
**Describe the problem**
Rounded gauges are taking a lot of space for no real reasons.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like an option to change the rounded gauge to horizontal bars, with values on the on left side. The inner gauge could be a thicker bar right below, stick to the main gauge (as it is in the actual rounded concept).

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Any way to have some visual with threashold colors, with main and inner values, but taking less space.

title: Inner setpoint needle incorrectly uses main gauge min/max limits for its position calculation
author: ansgar77
body: ### The problem I encountered an issue when using the inner gauge feature alongside an inner setpoint. If the main gauge and the inner gauge have different min and max values, the inner setpoint's needle calculates its physical position on the arc using the main gauge's min/max parameters, rather than its own inner parameters. Expected Behavior: The needle of the inner setpoint should calculate its position based on the min and max values defined within the inner object. Actual Behavior: The inner setpoint needle positions itself based on the main gauge's min/max scale. If the inner setpoint value falls outside the main gauge's limits, the needle simply clips to the far left or far right of the arc, ignoring the inner scale completely. ### Expectation Expected Behavior: The needle of the inner setpoint should calculate its position based on the min and max values defined within the inner object. ### YAML configuration (consider this required!) ```yaml type: custom:gauge-card-pro entity: sensor.test_battery min: -800 max: 3800 inner: min: -7000 max: 7000 setpoint: value: -6000 # EXPECTED: Needle appears near the left start of the arc (inner scale). # ACTUAL: Needle appears at the far left edge of the main scale because -6000 is far below the main min of -800. # if you set the value to zero you can see that see inner needle goes to the zero point of the main gauge. ``` ### Screenshots ### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information in the screenshot zero of the inner gauge is in the middle of the arc, the maingauge has zero at the right end of the left red segment - where the inner needle is pointing at but should be on the white needle in the middle

title: Orange tap circle appears on more info clicks
author: inventor7777
body:
### The problem

I have noticed this for about a year now, but never thought to make an issue.

When I tap on the values with my iPad or iPhone it happens 5/10 times, and 3/10 times on my Mac. Tested in HA Companion, Safari, and Opera. It does not clear until a full reload.

*The only card that experiences this is the Gauge Card Pro*

### Expectation

When I tap, there should be no strange orange circles.

### YAML configuration (consider this required!)

```yaml
type: custom:layout-card
      columns: 18
      rows: 3
    hold_action:
      action: more-info
    entity2: sensor.sr_a_c_plug_wattage
    min: 106
    max: 126
    inner:
      mode: severity
      min: 0
      max: 1800
      segments:
        - from: 0
          color: rgb(6,155,229)
    value_texts:
      primary:
        hold_action:
          action: more-info
        tap_action:
          action: more-info
        double_tap_action:
          action: more-info
          entity: climate.sr_ir_esp_sr_ac
      secondary:
        tap_action:
          action: more-info
        hold_action:
          action: navigate
          navigation_path: /dashboard-main/storeroom
    icons:
      right:
        type: template
        value: |-
          {{
                  {
                    "icon": "mdi:current-ac",
                    "label": (states('sensor.sr_a_c_plug_current') | float(0) | round(2)) ~ " A"
                  }
                }}
        tap_action:
          action: more-info
          entity: sensor.sr_a_c_plug_current
      left:
        type: template
        value: |-
          {{ {
            "icon": "mdi:chart-line",
            "label": "%.2f" | format(states('sensor.sr_a_c_plug_power_factor') | float(0))~ "%"
          } }}
        tap_action:
          action: more-info
          entity: sensor.sr_a_c_plug_power_factor
    feature_entity: climate.sr_ir_esp_sr_ac
    features:
      - type: climate-hvac-modes
        style: dropdown
        hvac_modes:
          - cool
          - dry
          - fan_only
          - "off"
      - type: climate-fan-modes
        style: dropdown
      - type: adjust-temperature
grid_options:
  columns: full
  rows: auto
```

### Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6fdf0ce9-388b-4ba9-8c08-dfec45ff5754

### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us?

```txt
No errors relating to GCP
```

_No response_

title: values not visible
author: Simonz82
body: ### The problem Hi, congratulations on the cards—I love them and they look great, but as you can see from the image, sometimes it doesn’t load the power consumption values at all; sometimes it’s the fridge, sometimes the oven, and sometimes it loads them late, etc. I also have a card that tracks temperatures, and it does the same thing—it loads slowly or doesn’t load the values at all. It’s the only card out of the many I have that behaves this way. How can I fix this? I tried uninstalling and reinstalling the component via HACS, but nothing changed. ### Expectation ... ### YAML configuration (consider this required!) ```yaml type: custom:gauge-card-pro entity: sensor.generale_power needle: true min: 0 max: 5000 segments: - from: 0 color: var(--blue-color) - from: 1000 color: var(--light-green-color) - from: 1500 color: var(--light-green-color) - from: 3000 color: var(--orange-color) - from: 4000 color: var(--red-color) gradient: true gradient_resolution: medium titles: primary: Generale icon: mdi:flash type: custom:gauge-card-pro entity: sensor.temp_salone_temperature entity2: sensor.temp_salone_humidity needle: true min: "12" max: "40" segments: - from: 12 color: var(--blue-color) - from: 18 color: var(--light-blue-color) - from: 24 color: var(--light-green-color) - from: 28 color: var(--light-green-color) - from: 31 color: var(--orange-color) - from: 38 color: var(--red-color) inner: min: 0 max: 100 mode: needle gradient: true gradient_resolution: auto segments: - from: 0 color: var(--red-color) - from: 40 color: var(--light-blue-color) - from: 60 color: var(--light-blue-color) - from: 100 color: var(--black-color) gradient: true gradient_resolution: auto value_texts: primary: value: "{{ states(entity) | float | round(1) }}°C" secondary: value: "{{ states(entity2) | float | round(0) }}%" color: "#aaa" titles: primary: value: Salone hide_background: false icons: right: type: battery value: sensor.temp_salone_battery hide_label: false ``` ### Screenshots _No response_ ### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information _No response_

Please describe.** I want humidity to align with temperature, cannot find option to reverse direction of second gauge. **Describe the solution you'd like** Ability to "reverse" direction of gauges so minimum value is on the right-hand side **Describe alternatives you've considered** N/A **Additional context** Image shows Temperature and Humidity, would like to reverse direction of humidity. **Example Code:** ``` type: custom:gauge-card-pro entity: sensor.avg_upstairs_temperature segments: - pos: 6 color: "#6BAFFF" - pos: 12 color: blue - pos: 18 color: "#6BCF14" - pos: 23 color: "#DDb550" - pos: 26 color: "#EC4600" needle: true gradient: true titles: primary: value: Upstairs entity2: sensor.avg_upstairs_humidity min: 0 gradient_resolution: auto inner: mode: needle gradient: true min: 0 max: 100 round: full segments: - pos: 0 color: "#EC4600" - pos: 25 color: "#DDb550" - pos: 45 color: "#9BFF64" - pos: 55 color: "#6BCF14" - pos: 70 color: blue - pos: 80 color: "#6BAFFF" gradient_resolution: auto round: "off" max: 40 value_texts: primary: font_size_reduction: 8.5 card_mod: style: | ha-card { margin-left: 10px !important; margin-right: 10px !important; box-shadow: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5) 0px 4px 8px !important; } ```

title: Add an option to display an outline and the color of that outline on the gauge needles
author: bensonrodney
body:
I've got a set of colors on my gauge gradients that I think suit the purpose well, however, some are parts of the band are dark and some are light. No matter what color I choose for the needles, they get lost in the parts of the gradient that have a similar brightness level. For example, light colored needles get lost in the light parts of the gradient and dark colored needles get lost in the darker parts of the gradient.

The default set of gauges in Home Assistant seem to solve this by adding a black border around the needle which makes it very clear as to where the needle is.

I've tried a wide range of colors and brightness for the needles and they still get lost in parts of the gauge gradient.

I love Gauge Card Pro and think you've done an amazing job with it. I'd happily continue to use it without the change proposed here. I just thought it might be helpful for some people.

It is only a problem when I use the custom config template card to set the segments using variables. I don't seem to be able to embed any YAML to insert the values. It shows no errors, the needle just stays on zero. ### Expectation The pointer continues to move or there is a way to use variables in the segment positions. ### YAML configuration (consider this required!) ```yaml type: custom:config-template-card variables: targetamber: states['input_number.test_target_amber'].state targetgreen: states['input_number.test_target_green'].state entities: - sensor.soc card: type: custom:gauge-card-pro min: 0 max: 100 segments: - from: 0 color: red - from: ${targetamber} color: orange - from: ${targetgreen} color: green needle: true gradient: false titles: primary: value: Battery Charge entity: sensor.soc ``` ### Screenshots ### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information _No response_

title: My Gauge Card Pro has become slow
author: Fred89210
body:
### The problem

Hello, since gauge-card-pro v2.4.3 _(the problem persists in v2.4.8.1)_, these cards take several seconds to display the main needle in the correct position and to show the state, and even longer to display the setpoint needle in the correct position.
If I reinstall v2.4.2 or earlier, these cards load instantly.
The same observation applies whether accessing HA locally or remotely, on a PC or in the Android App.

### Expectation

The needles and states should be displayed instantly, as in versions 2.4.2 and earlier.

### YAML configuration (consider this required!)

```yaml
type: custom:gauge-card-pro
segments:
  - pos: 0
    color: var(--green-color)
  - pos: 50
    color: rgb(255, 255, 0)
  - pos: 80
    color: "#FFA500"
  - pos: 100
    color: red
needle: true
gradient: true
titles:
  primary: TEST
gradient_resolution: auto
entity: sensor.system_monitor_utilisation_de_la_memoire
value_texts:
  primary: >-
    {% set mem = states('sensor.system_monitor_utilisation_de_la_memoire') |
    float(0) %} {{ mem | round(0) }}
  primary_unit: "%"
setpoint:
  type: number
  value: 30
  color: red
```

### Screenshots

This video shows the difference between the versions.
I want to clarify that the display times are not fixed, it may appear faster, but never as fast as with v2.4.2 and earlier versions.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7a30be6c-8736-4f1f-8e21-b8c6793dde68

### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us?

```txt

```

### Additional information

Home Assistant OS
Core: 2026.3.4
Supervisor: 2026.03.2
Operating System: 17.1
Frontend: 20260312.1
Raspberry Pi 5 8Go

title: Main value text clips with Michroma font after recent update
author: SimonStent
body: ### The problem After the latest update, the main center value is clipped on the left and right when using the Michroma font via card_mod. This worked correctly in previous versions of Gauge Card Pro. ### Expectation Value (e.g., 54) renders fully. ### YAML configuration (consider this required!) ```yaml type: custom:gauge-card-pro entity: sensor.corrected_water_level min: 0 max: 100 needle: true needle_color: "#1e1e2e" gradient: true segments: - from: 0 color: red - from: 25 color: "#FFA500" - from: 50 color: rgb(255, 255, 0) - from: 75 color: var(--green-color) gradient_resolution: high titles: primary: Water Level Percent primary_color: limegreen secondary: >- {{ (states('sensor.corrected_water_level') | float / 100 * 50000) | round(0) | int }} L secondary_color: "#aaaaaa" value_texts: primary_unit: "" primary_font_size_reduction: 0 hide_background: false card_mod: style: | ha-card { background: #1e1e2e !important; border-radius: 16px !important; overflow: hidden !important; position: relative; border: 2px solid black !important; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06), 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.7), inset 0 1px 2px rgba(255,255,255,0.05); } ha-card, ha-card * { font-family: "Michroma", sans-serif !important; } ``` ### Screenshots ### Anything in the browser console (Ctrl + Shift + J or Cmd + Option + J) that might be useful for us? ```txt ``` ### Additional information _No response_
